category_title: Confident students with articulation-based Spoken Language Disorders
minimal_context: The student is participating in a peer turn-and-talk activity about who inspires them following an interactive read-aloud of 'Rocket Writes a Story'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We just finished reading 'Rocket Writes a Story'. Now it is time to turn and talk with your group. Who is someone that inspires you?

STUDENT: I will start the conversation.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead.

STUDENT: I know my answer.

TUTOR: Tell us.

STUDENT: My sister. She sires [sic] me to get my homework done. My sister sires me [sic].

options.A: Ask the student what she notices about the book or ask questions such as how she knows it is nonfiction.
options.B: Offer a short synopsis and a direct question related to the text (e.g., "Rocket wants to write a story... what inspires him?").
options.C: Require the student to raise a hand and wait to be called on, selecting students out of order to juxtapose contributions.
options.D: Accurately model the correct pronunciation of the vocabulary word "inspires" for the student during the peer conversation.
